title: Unique Paths
description: There is a robot on an m x n grid. The robot is initially located at the top-left corner (grid[0][0]) and tries to move to the bottom-right corner (grid[m-1][n-1]). The robot can only move either down or right at any point in time. Given the two integers m and n, return the number of possible unique paths that the robot can take to reach the bottom-right corner.
thinking_questions: From any cell, you can only go right or down. How many paths reach cell (i,j)?
What are the base cases for the first row and first column?
Can you reduce 2D dp to 1D by processing row by row?

hints.L1: Paths to cell (i,j) = paths from above + paths from left. Build up from top-left corner.
hints.L2: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]. First row and column all = 1. Reduce to 1D: dp[j] += dp[j-1].
hints.L3:
# Unique Paths — O(m*n) time, O(n) space
# KEY INSIGHT: paths(i,j) = paths(i-1,j) + paths(i,j-1)
# Base: first row/col all 1 (only one way to reach each)

def uniquePaths(m, n):
    dp = [1] * n  # represents current row, init as first row

    for i in range(1, m):          # rows 1..m-1
        for j in range(1, n):      # cols 1..n-1
            dp[j] += dp[j-1]       # dp[j] = above (old dp[j]) + left (dp[j-1])

    return dp[n-1]
# WHY O(n) space: each row only needs previous row
# MATH SHORTCUT: C(m+n-2, m-1) — choose m-1 downs from m+n-2 moves

follow_up_questions: How does adding obstacles change the problem? (Unique Paths II)
Can you solve this recursively with memoization?
solution_notes: Grid DP. dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]. O(n) space by rolling array. Math: C(m+n-2, m-1).
